CAMPAIGN HEADQUARTERS 
8820 Foothill Blvd., Sunland, Co. 91040 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street Building 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Gentlemen: 
!.D. No. 770003' Walt Haley, Treasurer 
P.O. Box 989' Sun Valley, Ca. 91352 
TELEPHONE: (213)352-1479 
April 1, 1977 
I am writing to request your oplnlon and guidence on our campaign's 
reporting responsibilities for the enclosed piece of campaign literature. 
The enclosed letter was originated by the Citizens for Louis R. Nowell, 
ID #74584. 
All production and postage costs were paid by that committee 
and, of course, will be reported by them. 
A large portion of the letter, however, requests support for our candidate, 
Jim Peterson. 
Does our campaign have to report this as a contribution in kind? 
If so, do we have to report the total value of the production and postage 
costs, or a proportionate percentage? 
Please advise us so that we can meet our reporting responsibilities. 
Sincerely, 
Chris Nelson 
Campaign Manager 
~, 
lOS 
Printed by AA Instant Printing, 14960 Ventura Blvd., Sherman Oaks 91403 


'1l 
LOUIS R. NOWELL 
COUNCILMAN, DISTRICT O~ 
LOS ANGElES, CALIFORNIA 90012 
April 1, 1977 
Dear First District Neighbor, 
I want to personally thank you for the opportunity you gave me to serve as your 
representative on the Los Angeles City Council for the past 14 1/2 years. 
It's 
been a job that provided both rewards and frustrations. 
As I look back on those 
years, however, I am filled with a deep sense of satisfaction and accomplishment. 
Together, we fought the good fight and were able to make our City a better place 
for all of us. 
Now, as I prepare to step down as your City Councilman, I would like to share 
some thoughts with you about two key areas that affect you and your City govern-
ment. 
First, I would like to ask for your continued support. 
Rather than turn my back 
on City government, I would like to keep the knowledge and experience I have 
gained at City Hall working productively for you. 
I decided to become a candidate 
for City Controller after much thought beca~se I believe that only someone who has 
first-hand experience fighting City waste can truly be effective in that post. 
Over the years, I have repeatedly demonstrated that I am not afraid of a fight and 
with your vote, I can be the fighter that the taxpayers need in the Controller's 
office. 
Second, I know that right now, you are carefully considering the qualifications 
of all the candidates running to become your new City Councilman. 
As you can 
well imagine, I have been watching that race pretty closely myself and I am 
deeply disturbed by some of the things I am seeing. 
I don't want our seat on 
the council to be bought by special interests from outside our district ... but 
the current campaign finance reports reveal that there is a real danger of that 
happening. 
The candidate who reports that he has raised the most money, Bob 
Ronka, was also compelled, by law, to show that more than 99% of his contributions 
came from outside our First District. 
That's dangerous! 
While it's been a fact 
of political life that every candidate must raise campaign money and that some of 
that money will be from out-of-district contributors, we have never had, to my 
knowledge, a candidate in the First District so dependent on outside funds.

sections.question: A large portion of the letter, however, requests support for our candidate, Jim Peterson. Does our campaign have to report this as a contribution in kind? If so, do we have to report the total value of the production and postage costs, or a proportionate percentage?
sections.question_synthetic: The Jim Peterson campaign requests guidance on whether they must report as an in-kind contribution a campaign mailer produced and paid for by the Citizens for Louis R. Nowell committee that also advocates for their candidate, and if so, whether to report the full cost or a proportionate share.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a campaign treasurer, not an FPPC response. The enclosed campaign literature from Louis R. Nowell is provided as context. No FPPC conclusion or advice is present in this document.

embedding.summary: An incoming request from the Jim Peterson campaign asking the FPPC whether a mailer produced and paid for by the Citizens for Louis R. Nowell committee, which also includes content supporting Jim Peterson, must be reported as an in-kind contribution, and if so, how to value it for reporting purposes.
